metadata.author: Lexi Blackburn

basis.authority_chain: OWNER interview resolutions (2026-07-17) > vendor docs / verified benchmarks > seed hypotheses; no merge-level averaging.

crs.title: security_review #1: Opus (K=1 routing) vs Fable (K=2) vs Sol (K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: **RAW-capability ordering led by `codex/gpt-5.6-sol@max`** (CTF-capture 96.7% is the ONLY ≥2-hub [CORROBORATED] anchor in the category, kingy+semgrep), **but ROUTING PICK diverges to trusted `claude/claude-opus-4-8@max`.** Basis: defensive review is trust-gated; owner binds M1 (METR reward-hacking/ concealment) as a valid re-rank modifier → Sol gated OUT of the routing pick, magnitude untouched. Among trusted Anthropic options, Opus's placement is interpolation from trusted review-strength; Fable's only security signal is `[ASSUMPTION]` Mythos-proxy (single-hub, restricted, classifier-lifted) — a weaker basis to route DEFENSIVE review through. Opus is the lowest-variance trusted anchor (also the merged fallback pick, consistent). K=2's Fable-#1 rests on assumption-grade evidence; K=3's Sol-#1 under-weights the binding modifier + category trust-sensitivity. Residual uncertainty: **HIGH** — genuine 3-way split; the only corroborated anchor (Sol) is trust-gated out; Anthropic-native cyber data is a hard GAP.

crs.title: knowledge_synthesis #1: Fable (K=1/K=2) vs Sol (K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: **ROUTING `claude/claude-fable-5@max`; `codex/gpt-5.6-sol@max` elevated to CORROBORATED co-anchor #2** (K=1 had buried Sol at #7). Basis: Sol's GPQA-D 94.35 is [CORROBORATED] (AA+vellum) and Sol uniquely covers the long-context leg (MRCR 91.5 / BrowseComp 92.2) where Anthropic is a published GAP — so Sol is a legitimate co-anchor, elevated. BUT the routing default stays Fable because (a) Sol's knowledge profile is structurally NARROW — OpenAI omitted HLE/MMLU-Pro/classic academics at launch (vendor GAP), while Fable has the broader measured profile AND leads the synthesis-relevant research-augmented leg (HLE-w/tools 64.5 vs 52.2); (b) GPQA-D is ~tied (93.35 vs 94.35) and already cross-listed to math_proof where Sol wins — it should not double-count to also hand Sol synthesis; (c) M1 faithfulness caveat (concealment + confident-hallucination) is adverse for a synthesis default, gateable via citation-verification. Not a vote — a source-based split (corroborated co-anchor credited in the ordering; trusted broad model as routing default). Residual: **HIGH** — within-noise on the common axis; defensible either way; feeds Sol@max as retrieval/long-context specialist.

crs.title: data_analysis #1: Fable-proxy (K=1) vs Sonnet-5 (K=2) vs Sonnet-4.6 (K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: **ROUTING `claude/claude-sonnet-4-6@high`.** Basis: the ONLY direct current-gen category rows are Spider-2.0-Lite (Sonnet-5 50.6, Sonnet-4.6 49.6, Grok-4.3 45.9), all single vendor board [UNVERIFIED] + VLDB "broken bench" caveat (BIRD 52.8% / Spider-Snow 62.8% annotation-error). K=1's Fable-#1 is pure interpolation with NO direct row → weakest-sourced (rejected: proxy cannot outrank direct evidence). Between the two Sonnets: 50.6 vs 49.6 is WITHIN the bench's documented error bars (not a reliable discriminator), and Sonnet-5 is NOT a baseline routing member (single-tile evidence, appears nowhere else). Tie-break to the established baseline member → Sonnet-4.6@high. **Sonnet-5@high surfaced as higher-raw roster-expansion candidate** (dispatchable claude/, flag for roster add). Flagship interpolations (Fable/GPT-5.5/Opus) sit BELOW in an explicit [DATA_MISSING] baseline-fallback band (Q4-A). Residual: **VERY HIGH** — category effectively unmeasured; owner Phase-2 SQL pull queued.

crs.title:
composites (prompt_engineering / vulnerability_research / molecular_biology): Fable (K=1/K=2) vs Sol
(K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: recomposed from MERGED parents only (rule: composites from parents). prompt_engineering → **Fable@max** (leads qr + ks-routing parents; Sol@max close #2 lifted by mechanical lead; Fable's mechanical leg is [DATA_MISSING], noted). vulnerability_research → RAW composite led by **Sol@max** (corroborated CTF security parent at 0.50 weight) but **ROUTING diverges to trusted `claude/claude-fable-5@max`** (corroborated debugging+coding parents 95.0, no adverse flag; Sol trust-gated — M1 maximal for exploit research; Opus@max alternate ultra-conservative trusted pick). molecular_biology → **Fable@max** (leads top-weighted ks-routing + data_analysis parents; Sol@max very-close #2 on corroborated math parent). Basis: K=3's Sol-led composites are an artifact of K=3's Sol-led parents (security/ks); once parents are merged (CR-1, CR-2), the composites recompute to Fable routing with Sol as raw-capability co-leader/#2. Residual: **HIGH** (mol-bio + prompt_eng flip on parent-weight within noise; recorded).

crs.title: Haiku rankability: proxies (K=2) vs unrankable (K=1/K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: **Haiku EXCLUDED from every performance-branch ordering.** Two independent binding reasons: (a) performance branch requires ≥high effort and Haiku exposes NO selectable effort rung (cannot express high/xhigh/max; @null is not ≥high); (b) Haiku has ZERO cited benchmark rows in the corpus (A4 gap-10) → any placement is uncited baseline-holdover, violating citation-validity. K=2's @null-floor rows are rejected on both counts. Haiku's home is the cost_efficiency branch (out of scope), consistent with baseline (Haiku appears ONLY in cost_efficiency.*). Residual: **LOW**.

crs.title: GPT-5.6 effort ladder: high/max vs xhigh exclusion.
crs.resolution: Resolution (CONSENSUS across all three judges): Sol = **{high, max}**; Terra/Luna = **{high}**; **xhigh EXCLUDED** (A1 spec vs A5 conflict unresolved) — emission must confirm on platform.openai.com; if confirmed, insert Sol@xhigh between max and high in every Sol ordering (monotone). "Ultra" = Sol-only multi-agent COMPUTE MODE (TB2.1 91.9 / 74.3), NOT an effort rung → context-only, never a pairing. Residual: **LOW-MODERATE**.

crs.title: agentic_execution Sol demotion depth.
crs.resolution: Resolution: Fable band #1–3 (corroborated multi-signal, no flag); GPT-5.5 #4–5 (only DOM primary-board-verified TB2.0 84.7±2.1); Terra@high preferred non-Anthropic agentic seat (tool-call reliability 97.2, no flag); **Sol demoted below the trusted/verified cluster** — M1 is STRONGEST here (reward-hacking observed DURING the long-horizon agentic eval) AND Sol carries the lowest tool-call reliability (91.4 vs Terra 97.2), a separate magnitude concern. Blends K=1 (Terra>Sol) with the one-band-modifier reading. Grok note-only (M2 exfiltration — the exact agentic-tooling risk surface). Residual: MODERATE.

crs.title: coding/mechanical #1 effort (max K=1 vs high K=2/K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: performance-branch orderings are capability-monotone → **@max first** (coding #1 = Fable@max; mechanical #1 = Sol@max). The "95.0 is effort-insensitive, @high = best price-perf" insight is TRUE but belongs to the **cost_efficiency branch** (fed by the cost figures emitted here), not a performance re-order. Residual: LOW (Fable capability delta across its effort band is within noise).

crs.title: provider tags: openai/* (K=1/K=2) vs codex/* + api/* (K=3).
crs.resolution: Resolution: normalize GPT-5.6-* to `codex/*` (dispatchable via existing OpenAI adapter); open-weight/Qwen/Grok under `api/*` = advisory-only pending adapter wiring. See provider-mapping note. Residual: LOW (emission-phase mechanical).

crs.title: fallback_default@99: Opus (K=1/K=3) vs Sonnet-4.6 (K=2).
crs.resolution: Resolution: **`claude/claude-opus-4-8@high`** — trusted (no adverse flag), balanced, mid-cost $5/$25, corroborated general capability (SWE-V 88.1, SWE-Pro 69.2), top-3 across coding/debug/synthesis. K=2's Sonnet-4.6 (cheapest-with-a-row) is the cheaper alternate but a weaker generalist. Residual: LOW.

crs.title: roster-expansion candidates surfaced (not core, advisory).
crs.resolution: Opus-4.7 (K=3 debugging/coding via SWE-V mean 84.8) and Sonnet-5 (data_analysis) are current-gen-retained but NOT baseline routing members. Surfaced as advisory roster-expansion candidates; not injected into core dispatchable orderings except Sonnet-5 flagged in data_analysis (CR-3). Residual: LOW.

crs.title: Qwen3.7-Max status.
crs.resolution: Closed API model (no HF checkpoint; Q3 named-checkpoint rule is about open-weight quant ambiguity and does not bar a closed model, but it is single-hub [UNVERIFIED], not in baseline, needs api adapter). Kept advisory-only, capped LC, where it has a cited number (math HMMT 97.1, mechanical IFEval 94.3). Never a routing pick. Residual: LOW. ---

performance_cost_winsorization.note: Performance-branch only: per-pairing $/token clipped to the [p05,p95] universe window before cost_norm (renormalized by the winsorized max), so an extreme price ratio cannot swing the capability-dominant score (~R^0.2). cost_efficiency uses the raw cost_norm.

metadata.cost_figure_methodology: SOP-2 worst-case $/token: constant 100K-in / 20K-visible-out blend, opus tokenizer inflation 1.35x (1.4x deprecated), effort hidden-output multiplier ladder, gpt-5.5 272K cliff sub-cliff at this blend. cost_norm = $/token / universe-max in (0,1]. Branch score = perf_norm^a / cost_norm^b. Refinement #6: the PERFORMANCE branch winsorizes $/token to the [p05,p95] universe window before cost_norm (see performance_cost_winsorization) so an extreme price ratio cannot swing the capability-dominant score; cost_efficiency uses the raw cost_norm.

formula_definitions.performance_branch_tiebreak: same-model equal measured perf_norm → effort descending (max-effort first) before cost can discriminate. Cost separates only across models on the performance branch. (#1 fix)
formula_definitions.performance_branch_ordering: performance_rank_pin rows sort first, then phase-2 synthesis ranks where cited, then power-law branch score.
